production_mt.source: FAOSTAT global potato production 2022 (374.8 Mt); China 95.5 Mt (25.5%), India 56 Mt (15.0%)

density_g_per_cm3.source: raw potato specific gravity ~1.05-1.09 (potatoes sink in water)

tier1_fates.name: Fresh / table food
tier1_fates.source: CIP: fresh consumption ~2/3 of harvest; total food (fresh+processed) ~2/3, of which fresh is the bulk

tier1_fates.name: Processing (food)
tier1_fates.source: FAO: processing 10% of production in 2013 and rising; China ~15%, India ~7%, NL/US 55-60%; global weighted ~14%

tier1_fates.name: Animal feed
tier1_fates.source: FAO FBS utilisation accounts; large share of crop used as feed (culls/surplus)

tier1_fates.name: Seed (replanting)
tier1_fates.source: FAO FBS; potatoes have a high seed rate (tubers held back for planting)

tier1_fates.name: Waste & loss (accounted)
tier1_fates.source: FAO FBS 'losses' line (post-harvest accounted losses; distinct from full-chain FLW overlay)

tier1_fates.name: Industrial (non-food)
tier1_fates.source: native/modified starch, alcohol (vodka/aquavit), bioplastics; larger in developed producers

tier2_processing.name: Frozen (fries/wedges/tots)
tier2_processing.source: frozen (fries-led) is the largest processed potato category globally; big-three McCain/Lamb Weston/Simplot

fries_within_frozen_pct.source: fries dominate frozen potato products; remainder wedges/tots/hash browns

food_loss_waste_overlay.note: Cross-cutting overlay along field->consumer chain. NOT summed into tier1_fates (would double-count with the FBS 'losses' line). Reported as the Conscience Number.

chain_loss_pct.source: Roots & tubers have the highest FLW of any commodity group (>25%); regional range 33% (N.Africa/W&C Asia) to 60% (N.America/Oceania). Potato harvest often 'reduced by half from field to fork'.
